summary: Conjecture 3.6, which asserts that any permutation π satisfying cyc(σ)=2 for every σ∈πC_n must have n prime and be of the affine form π_{n,a}, is disproved by Bastide, Bishnoi, Groenland, Gijswijt, and Joshi (arXiv:2510.18529). The smallest counterexample occurs at n=23: a specific non-affine permutation satisfies t([π])=21=n−2 yet is not of the form π_{n,a}; all counterexamples found have the structure of quadratic orthomorphisms. The implied Conjecture 3.4 (t(n)=n−2 iff n is prime) remains open but is further supported.

since_posted.title: Circular sorting, strong complete mappings and wreath product constructions
since_posted.authors: Paul Bastide, Anurag Bishnoi, Carla Groenland, Dion Gijswijt, Rohinee Joshi
since_posted.claim: Disproves Conjecture 3.6 by exhibiting non-affine quadratic orthomorphisms achieving t([π])=n−2 for prime n, with the smallest counterexample an explicit permutation of length n=23.

search_queries: Adin Alon Roichman "circular sorting" conjecture permutations cyclic 2502.14398
circular sorting permutation "cyc" cyclic group prime characterization arXiv 2025
notes: Conjecture 3.6 is a structural strengthening of Conjecture 3.4; its disproof does not immediately disprove 3.4. arXiv:2510.18529 also provides evidence for Conjecture 3.4 (t(n)≤n−3 when n is composite) and proves a tight upper bound for n=25 via exhaustive search using strong complete mappings.
